Name: hardware_optimized_inference
Title: Optimizing Neural Network Inference on Commodity GPUs through Hardware-Aware Quantization and Sparse-Aware DVFS
Short Hypothesis: By combining hardware-aware quantization techniques with sparse-aware dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS), we can achieve significant improvements in inference efficiency on commodity GPUs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as APNAS, HLQ, and SparseDVFS have explored hardware-aware neural network optimization, quantization, and DVFS for efficient inference. However, our proposal combines these techniques in a novel way to achieve optimal performance on commodity GPUs.
Abstract: This project proposes a novel approach to optimizing neural network inference on commodity GPUs through the combination of hardware-aware quantization and sparse-aware DVFS. By leveraging the strengths of both techniques, we aim to achieve significant improvements in inference efficiency without sacrificing accuracy. Our approach will involve the development of a quantization-aware training framework that takes into account the hardware characteristics of the target GPU, as well as a sparse-aware DVFS scheme that adaptively adjusts the frequency and voltage of the GPU based on the sparsity of the neural network. We will evaluate our approach on a range of benchmark datasets and compare its performance to state-of-the-art methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the performance of our approach on a range of benchmark datasets, including ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our approach to state-of-the-art methods, including APNAS and SparseDVFS
Conduct ablation studies to determine the contribution of each component of our approach to the overall performance improvement
Measure the energy efficiency of our approach using metrics such as power consumption and energy delay product
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the quantization-aware training framework and sparse-aware DVFS scheme may require significant computational resources and expertise to implement
The approach may not be effective for all types of neural networks or datasets
The energy efficiency of the approach may vary depending on the specific hardware and system configuration used
